summary: Country‑infused set with standout “Doin’ That Rag” and “High Time”.
blurb: The June 28 1969 show delivers a tight country‑rock groove, highlighted by the pedal‑steel intro into “Doin' That Rag” that flows straight into an exuberant “High Time.” Jerry’s lead work, Phil’s rocking bass, and crisp dual drumming keep the set rolling with plenty of folk‑blues flavor.
review: Recorded on June 28, 1969 at Santa Rosa’s Veterans Auditorium, this early Dead show marks a decisive turn toward the Working Man’s Dead sound. The band rides a bright country‑rock wave, opening with “Slewfoot” and weaving through classics like “Silver Threads And Golden Needles,” “Mama Tried,” and “Me And My Uncle.” The centerpiece is the seamless segue from the steel‑guitar driven “Doin' That Rag” into a rollicking “High Time,” where Jerry’s pedal‑steel intro and soaring licks sit perfectly with Bob’s youthful vocal bite. Phil’s bass is described as “rocking like it’s a baby in his arms,” while Mickey and Bill provide firecracker‑like double‑drum drive that propels the jam forward. Tom’s organ adds a quirky, ‘mad scientist’ texture throughout, giving the set an extra layer of intrigue. With solid soundboard capture—clear levels, minor hiss but no drop‑outs—the recording lets listeners hear every nuance of this pivotal moment in Dead history.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: Both recordings are described with unequivocal positive language—'stellar', 'excellent', 'crisp, clear audio' and multiple reviewers praise the same songs (e.g., Doin' That Rag, High Time) and band performances. Consistent commendation of Jerry’s pedal‑steel work, Phil’s bass, Bob’s vocals, Tom’s organ, and the dual drums indicates strong positive sentiment across sources.

best_recording.reason: Provides a solid soundboard capture with clear levels and only minor hiss; includes all songs and highlights the praised pedal‑steel intro and tight transitions.

key_highlights: Pedal‑steel intro into “Doin' That Rag” leading directly into an energetic “High Time.”
Strong country‑rock selections like “Slewfoot,” “Silver Threads And Golden Needles,” and “Mama Tried.”
Tom’s quirky organ work adds texture, while the dual drums drive the set with firecracker energy.

band_performance.Jerry: Delivers a standout pedal‑steel intro on “Doin’ That Rag,” ripping licks on “Sittin’ On Top Of The World,” and powerful vocals on “Me & My Uncle.”
band_performance.Phil: Bass is described as rocking like a baby in his arms, providing a solid, supportive foundation throughout the set.
band_performance.Bob: Vocals are youthful and cut to the core, meshing perfectly with Jerry’s lead on the cowboy‑tuned numbers.
band_performance.Tom: Organ adds a ‘mad scientist’ character, supplying quirky texture that enhances the country‑rock vibe.
band_performance.Mickey: Double‑drum magic highlighted; his playing is firecracker‑like and drives the grooves forward.
band_performance.Billy: Provides steady, propulsive drumming that locks in with Mickey for a powerful rhythmic backbone.
